content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 14, 2003


Doreathea Johnson, Deputy Director
Department of Consumer Affairs, Legal Affairs
400 R Street, Suite 3090
Sacramento, CA 95814-6200

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-117

Dear Ms. Johnson:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the California Department of Consumer Affairs regarding the conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ In the process of preparing a response to your letter we determined that the questions you raised are better addressed in the context of a review of your agency's conflict of interest code.² Thus, we are withdrawing the advice request and will work with the Commission's Technical Assistance Division in responding to your request in the context of a review of your code.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\03-117

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² The Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

sections.question_synthetic: The Department of Consumer Affairs requested advice on conflict of interest code provisions under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is withdrawing the advice request and will address the questions instead through a review of the agency's conflict of interest code in coordination with the Commission's Technical Assistance Division.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no formal Q/C sections. Questions were preliminary/not formally structured. FPPC determined advice request better handled as code review rather than written advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The Department of Consumer Affairs requested advice on conflict of interest code provisions under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is withdrawing the advice request and will address the questions instead through a review of the agency's conflict of interest code in coordination with the Commission's Technical Assistance Division.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdraws an advice request from the Department of Consumer Affairs regarding conflict of interest code provisions, determining that the questions are better addressed through a formal code review process.
